prompt:
You are a blind ceiling-quality judge. You receive one task and three anonymized candidate patches. Do not infer or mention model, arm, attempt, harness, or author identity; judge only the task text and diffs.

Task text:
AlterOrderWithRespectTo() with ForeignKey crash when _order is included in Index().
Description
	
	class Meta:
		db_table = 'look_image'
		order_with_respect_to = 'look'
		indexes = [
			models.Index(fields=['look', '_order']),
			models.Index(fields=['created_at']),
			models.Index(fields=['updated_at']),
		]
migrations.CreateModel(
			name='LookImage',
			fields=[
				('id', models.AutoField(auto_created=True, primary_key=True, serialize=False, verbose_name='ID')),
				('look', models.ForeignKey(on_delete=django.db.models.deletion.CASCADE, related_name='images', to='posts.Look', verbose_name='LOOK')),
				('image_url', models.URLField(blank=True, max_length=10000, null=True)),
				('image', models.ImageField(max_length=2000, upload_to='')),
				('deleted', models.DateTimeField(editable=False, null=True)),
				('created_at', models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)),
				('updated_at', models.DateTimeField(auto_now=True)),
			],
		),
		migrations.AddIndex(
			model_name='lookimage',
			index=models.Index(fields=['look', '_order'], name='look_image_look_id_eaff30_idx'),
		),
		migrations.AddIndex(
			model_name='lookimage',
			index=models.Index(fields=['created_at'], name='look_image_created_f746cf_idx'),
		),
		migrations.AddIndex(
			model_name='lookimage',
			index=models.Index(fields=['updated_at'], name='look_image_updated_aceaf9_idx'),
		),
		migrations.AlterOrderWithRespectTo(
			name='lookimage',
			order_with_respect_to='look',
		),
I added orders_with_respect_to in new model class's Meta class and also made index for '_order' field by combining with other field. And a new migration file based on the model looks like the code above.
The problem is operation AlterOrderWithRespectTo after AddIndex of '_order' raising error because '_order' field had not been created yet.
It seems to be AlterOrderWithRespectTo has to proceed before AddIndex of '_order'.

+    def _get_dependencies_for_generated_fields(
+        self, app_label, model_name, field_names, expressions=()
+    ):
+        """Make generated fields available before database operations use them."""
+        model = self.new_apps.get_model(app_label, model_name)

+                fields.update(
+                    field_name
+                    for field_name, *_ in model._get_expr_references(expression)

+                isinstance(operation, migrations.AddIndex) and
+                operation.index.fields == ["look", "_order"]
+            )
+        )

Ties are allowed only by putting indistinguishable packets in the same tier. For every strict winner-loser pair created by tiers, include exactly one concrete one-sentence delta citing something visible in the diffs. Output ONLY the JSON object -- no prose before or after, no markdown fences.

strict_win_deltas.delta: P2 adds explicit dependency tuples (app_label, model_name, '_order', True) resolved via a new AlterOrderWithRespectTo branch in the dependency-satisfaction check, staying inside the autodetector's existing dependency-graph idiom, whereas P1 achieves ordering purely by relocating generate_altered_order_with_respect_to() earlier in the top-level call sequence in _detect_changes and relying on insertion-order tie-breaking in the topological sort.

P2 factors index/constraint field-and-expression detection into one reusable _get_dependencies_for_generated_fields() helper wired into generate_added_indexes, generate_added_constraints, and both CreateModel loops, whereas P3 builds a bespoke _index_references_order() helper plus a new 'order_wrt_set' dependency-type string that is wired only into the index code paths.

P1's single reorder of two code blocks uniformly protects both the CreateModel indexes loop and the constraints loop with one change, whereas P3 introduces a new dependency-type sentinel and helper but never touches generate_added_constraints or the CreateModel constraints loop, leaving that path structurally unaddressed.

P2's helper also inspects index.condition and constraint.check/condition via model._get_expr_references(), so an Index(condition=Q(_order__gte=0)) or CheckConstraint(check=Q(_order__gte=0)) is correctly ordered after '_order' is created, a case P1 never detects and only happens to satisfy because its reorder is unconditional rather than reasoned about field references.

P2 adds '_order' dependency tracking to generate_added_constraints and the CreateModel constraints loop via the same helper, while P3's '_index_references_order' check and 'order_wrt_set' dependency are consulted only inside generate_created_models's index loop and generate_added_indexes, leaving AddConstraint operations referencing '_order' unprotected against the same crash.

P1's reorder in generate_created_models unconditionally emits AlterOrderWithRespectTo before both the index loop and the 'for constraint in constraints' loop, so any constraint referencing '_order' is also safely ordered, whereas P3 special-cases only AddIndex operations and leaves AddConstraint operations for the same model with no '_order'-aware dependency at all.

P2's tests test_set_alter_order_with_respect_to_before_condition_index_constraint and test_add_model_order_with_respect_to_condition_index_constraint pin down behavior for Q(_order__gte=0)-style expression references, a regression net P1's test_add_model_order_with_respect_to_index_constraint and test_set_order_with_respect_to_index_constraint_together never assert.

P2's added tests assert exact operation ordering for both AddIndex and AddConstraint across create and alter paths, whereas P3's test_set_alter_order_with_respect_to_index and test_add_model_order_with_respect_to_index cover indexes only and add no constraint-referencing-'_order' regression test at all.

P1's test_set_order_with_respect_to_index_constraint_together exercises order_with_respect_to combined with unique_together, index_together, indexes, and constraints simultaneously and asserts the full resulting operation order, a broader combined-option surface than P3's index-only tests which never touch constraints, unique_together, or index_together.

P2 keeps the fix entirely inside dependency-declaration code (one new helper plus one new elif branch) without touching the master method-call sequence in _detect_changes, whereas P1 permanently relocates self.generate_altered_order_with_respect_to() in that sequence, a global reordering whose rationale isn't localized to the '_order'-referencing-index case it addresses.

P2 exposes one general-purpose helper (_get_dependencies_for_generated_fields) callable from all four index/constraint generation sites, whereas P3 spreads the fix across a new 'order_wrt_set' string literal, a new _index_references_order static method, and duplicated old/new-model-state lookups inside generate_added_indexes.

P1's change is confined to relocating two already-existing code blocks with no new dependency-type vocabulary, whereas P3 adds a new magic string 'order_wrt_set' as a dependency-tuple sentinel alongside the pre-existing True/'order_wrt_unset' conventions, growing the implicit protocol the dependency-resolution code must handle.
